text:
Once upon a time in the sleepy town of Quiet Creek, there lived an old recluse named Beatrice Halligan. She had lived there since the dawn of time and nobody knew much about her except for her love for antique clocks.

One peculiar afternoon, a visitor came—a scholar named Dr. Percival Thornbourne. He had heard tales of the clock-maker's talent and decided to visit. Beatrice received him with a mixture of surprise and polite dignity.

Dr. Thornbourne explained his interest in clocks—a passion he felt was rare.

"What's special about these?" he asked Beatrice.

"They tell time...just like us."

Beatrice chuckled heartily.

"I've got a cuckoo clock. Watch it."

She wound him up and soon there was a cheerful cuckoo singing its tune. Impressed, Dr. Thornbourne asked her about time kept clocks.

"They're ingenious," she confided. "But why?"

"A secret?"

"For you."

She hummed a bit.
